purpose: Pre-committed clamp check (2026-07-24 ruling): visual bad rate on the first ~64 clamped outputs must fall CLEARLY below the 17.5% D1 baseline (expect single digits), else KILL the array and report the taxonomy without iterating clamps blind.
method: 32 accepted target clips + the 16 most offender-enriched accepted reference clips, judged from 16-frame filmstrips (t = 0,4,8,14,22,32,44,56,68,80,92,100,108,112,116,120) tiled into labelled contact sheets. Judgement by the operator (Claude) on the rendered frames; every clip listed had already PASSED the frozen gate (tau 0.2543 / max_pure<=0.5 / seam<=2.0 / dq<=0.5).

sample_design.note: deliberate oversample per the ruling, so the raw chunk rate is NOT directly comparable to the 17.5% baseline; the offender/non-offender split and the uniform projection below are.

classes.BAD: a frame is destroyed: near-black/black edge-map, white blowout, blank frame, or a saturated flat colour dominating the frame
classes.MARGINAL: clearly tinted / washed but the scene stays legible, or an intentional light-leak style
classes.OK: transition reads as a transition on real footage

d2f_0018: orange burn front covers much of frame
d2f_0025: red/magenta flash dominates; uPow=12.9992 and uSpreadClr=[2.61,0.23,0.96] BYPASSED the clamp (see clamp_gaps)
d2f_0030: white blowout; CAUGHT by the non-gating m1_min_flag (m1_min=-0.240, dq=0.434)

verdict: FAIL — pre-committed check not met. Raw chunk 25.0% BAD (targets) vs the 17.5% baseline; and critically the NON-OFFENDER subset alone is 15.8% and the uniform-allocation projection is 16.7% — i.e. essentially AT the baseline, not 'clearly below' and nowhere near single digits. The oversampling confound does NOT rescue it. Array 9659414 KILLED (with its dependent encode/assemble/audit chain) per the pre-committed rule.

diagnosis_the_clamp_caps_are_the_WRONG_S.mechanism: For a default d, rule 2 takes [0.5d, 2d] INTERSECT |v| <= 3.0. For EdgeTransition.edge_brightness (d = 8.0) that is [4,16] & <=3 = EMPTY, and for ColourDistance.power (d = 5.0) it is [2.5,10] & <=3 = {3.0}. The absolute cap therefore wins for 100% of draws INCLUDING the canonical default, collapsing both parameters to the constant 3.0 and DELETING their healthy range rather than trimming the tail.
diagnosis_the_clamp_caps_are_the_WRONG_S.EdgeTransition: edge_brightness 8.0 -> 3.0 always. LOWER brightness = DIMMER edge map = the near-black frames. Observed 4/4 target draws and 4/4 reference draws BAD-or-MARGINAL. The clamp converted 'occasionally blown at 20.3' into 'consistently dark at 3.0'. Brightness needs a FLOOR, not a ceiling.
diagnosis_the_clamp_caps_are_the_WRONG_S.ColourDistance: power 5.0 -> 3.0 always. LOWER power widens the colour-distance region = WHITE blowout (refs 0002, 0010). Same wrong direction.
diagnosis_the_clamp_caps_are_the_WRONG_S.measured_clamp_efficacy_note: First-attempt gate pass was slightly HIGHER for clamped draws (0.885) than unclamped (0.836), so the clamp is not hurting the metric gate — the failures it leaves are exactly the ones M1/M2/seam cannot see.

clamp_gaps_found.abbreviated_names_bypass_the_classifier: dissolve.uPow = 12.9992 was NOT clamped: 'uPow' does not contain 'power'. dissolve.uSpreadClr = [2.6137, 0.2273, 0.9565] and dissolve.uHotClr were NOT clamped: 'Clr' does not contain 'color'/'colour', and the fallback 'any 3-vector in [0,1]' rule cannot fire because 2.6137 > 1. Result: the single worst non-offender clip in the chunk.
clamp_gaps_found.luma_admits_saturated_colour: luma = 0.2126R+0.7152G+0.0722B in [0.15,0.85] constrains BRIGHTNESS, not chroma. Pure green [0.048,0.919,0.127] has luma 0.67 and is admitted, then dominates 4 frames. The originally flagged fadecolor value [0.40,0.36,0.25] has luma 0.36 and was ALREADY legal, so rule 3 never addressed the reported example.
clamp_gaps_found.untouched_failure_class: white blowout / blank frame from geometry-or-speed params (SimpleZoom.zoom_quickness, TilesWave.tileCount) is not a power/colour/relative-tail problem and no clamp rule addresses it. The non-gating m1_min_flag did catch SimpleZoom (m1_min = -0.240).

what_was_NOT_done: No clamp iteration (explicitly forbidden without a ruling). param_clamp.py is unchanged from the ruled specification.
